full_content:
Batch formalization: Sessions must proactively engage the user and initiate wrap-up guidance without requiring an explicit wrap-up instruction.

Owner directive:
- The user should not have to explicitly instruct GT-KB to perform session wrap-up procedures.
- Each session should actively inform and engage the user to draw attention to priorities across all dimensions of the project.
- Session interaction should simplify user input by suggesting actions and priorities.
- The owner clarified that the intended spelling is "priorities".

Artifacts to create:
- DELIB-0841: owner decision establishing proactive session lifecycle engagement and wrap-up guidance.
- GOV-SESSION-LIFECYCLE-PROACTIVE-ENGAGEMENT-001: governance requirement for sessions to actively present priorities and suggested actions.
- PB-SESSION-WRAP-UP-PROACTIVE-001: protected behavior requiring proactive wrap-up prompts/reports instead of waiting for manual wrap-up requests.
- DCL-SESSION-WRAP-UP-AUTOMATION-SAFETY-001: design constraint distinguishing safe automatic wrap-up reporting from mutating wrap-up operations that still require applicable approval or owner-authorized automation scope.

Implementation status:
- Session startup now writes and can emit a startup report.
- Session stop/wrap-up now writes and can emit a proactive wrap-up report.
- Claude settings and Codex hook intent register SessionStart and Stop lifecycle hooks where supported.
- Regression tests verify the startup dashboard, wrap-up report, hook intent, and release-gate wiring.
explicit_change_request: Owner stated the guiding principle that GT-KB should proactively perform session wrap-up engagement and draw attention to project priorities, then clarified the spelling as priorities.
change_reason: Formalize proactive session lifecycle engagement and wrap-up guidance.
